_notes: pitchScore 0-10. Higher = more likely vendor pitch. Hand-curated from LLM training knowledge of each company. Categories: saas-devtool, saas-other, consultancy, bigco-case-study, foundation, academia, agency, conference-org. The 'products' note describes what the company sells; runtime classifier bumps the score when the product is mentioned in the talk title/description.

Apify.products: Web-scraping platform with MCP-driven Agent Skills marketplace

GitLab.products: DevOps platform — repos, CI/CD, Duo Agent Platform

Gitbook.products: Hosted documentation platform with AI features

Tauri.products: Open-source desktop app framework + Crab Nebula commercial offerings

Crab Nebula.products: Commercial backers of Tauri; auto-updates, signing services

AUTO1 Group.products: European used-car marketplace; not a B2B dev-tools vendor

Booking.com.products: Travel platform; engineering talks are internal case studies

Cerbos.products: Open-source authorization engine + Cerbos Hub managed service

Cloudflare.products: CDN, edge compute (Workers), R2/D1, AI inference, Zero Trust suite

Cloudsmith.products: Universal package management SaaS (npm, Maven, Docker, etc.)

Directus.products: Open-core headless CMS / data platform with Directus Cloud

Harness.products: AI-native CI/CD, feature flags, chaos, FinOps platform; explicitly used as 'reference implementation' in their conference talk

Miro.products: Collaboration whiteboard SaaS; less dev-tool-focused but product-led

Nationale-Nederlanden.products: Dutch insurer; engineering talks are internal case studies

Rabobank.products: Dutch cooperative bank; engineering talks are internal case studies

Sentry.products: Error monitoring, performance and session replay SaaS

Storyblok.products: Headless CMS with visual editor + recently launched MCP server

Tesla.products: EV/automotive; engineering talks are internal case studies

The New York Times.products: Media; engineering talks are internal case studies

TomTom.products: Navigation/maps platform; offers Maps APIs but talks here are case studies
